questions.q: In the Ramayana, who is the supreme exemplar of bhakti (loving devotion)?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is celebrated above all as the eternal model of selfless devotion to Rama.

questions.q: To whom is Hanuman's loving devotion directed in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's devotion is wholly given to Rama, whom he serves selflessly.

questions.q: Bhakti is best described as the path of what?
questions.explanation: Bhakti is the path of loving devotion to the divine.

questions.q: What is Hanuman by species in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is a vanara, one of the monkey people who aid Rama.

questions.q: Hanuman is famously the son of which wind deity?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is the son of Vayu, the wind god, and is often called Pavanaputra.

questions.q: Hanuman crossed the ocean to find which captive of Ravana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman leapt across the sea to Lanka to locate the captive Sita.

questions.q: On which island kingdom did Hanuman find Sita held captive?
questions.explanation: Sita was held in Ravana's kingdom of Lanka, where Hanuman found her.

questions.q: What token did Rama give Hanuman to prove to Sita that he was Rama's true messenger?
questions.explanation: Rama gave Hanuman his signet ring to show Sita as a sign of trust.

questions.q: Devotion like Hanuman's is centered above all on what attitude toward Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's bhakti is expressed through humble, selfless service to Rama.

questions.q: Hanuman is a devoted minister and friend in the kingdom of which vanara king?
questions.explanation: Hanuman serves Sugriva, the vanara king of Kishkindha, before devoting himself to Rama.

questions.q: What is the name of the vanara kingdom where Hanuman first meets Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman first meets Rama and Lakshmana near Kishkindha, the vanara kingdom.

questions.q: Which mountain did Hanuman lift and carry to bring the life-saving Sanjivani herb?
questions.explanation: Hanuman carried the entire Dronagiri mountain bearing the Sanjivani herb to heal Lakshmana.

questions.q: When he first approached Rama and Lakshmana, in what guise did Hanuman appear?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, sent by Sugriva, first approached Rama disguised as a humble mendicant brahmin.

questions.q: Whom did Hanuman serve as a messenger and counselor before pledging himself to Rama?
questions.explanation: Hanuman was the trusted minister of Sugriva, who sent him to assess Rama.

questions.q: After delivering Rama's message, what did Hanuman do to Ravana's pleasure-grove in Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman uprooted and devastated the Ashoka grove where Sita was held to provoke the rakshasas.

questions.q: How did Hanuman set fire to Lanka after being captured?
options: With a torch from the palace
questions.explanation: When the rakshasas set his tail ablaze, Hanuman used it to burn much of Lanka.

questions.q: Which sea-monster tried to swallow Hanuman during his great leap to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Simhika, a shadow-seizing demoness of the sea, tried to seize Hanuman, who slew her.

questions.q: Which mother of the serpents tested Hanuman by demanding to swallow him during his ocean crossing?
questions.explanation: Surasa, mother of the nagas, tested Hanuman, who outwitted her by shrinking and passing through her mouth.

questions.q: Whom did Hanuman heal by fetching the Sanjivani herb during the war?
questions.explanation: Hanuman brought the Sanjivani herb to revive the gravely wounded Lakshmana.

questions.q: When Hanuman finally found Sita, where in Lanka was she being kept?
questions.explanation: Sita was held under guard in the Ashoka grove, where Hanuman discovered her.

questions.q: Which vanara elder reminded Hanuman of his forgotten powers so he could leap to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Jambavan, the wise bear-king, reawakened Hanuman to his vast strength before the leap.

questions.q: What is a common epithet for Hanuman reflecting his parentage by the wind?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is called Pavanaputra, meaning son of the wind (Pavana/Vayu).

questions.q: In the Sundara Kanda, after burning Lanka, what was Hanuman's chief worry once the flames spread?
options: That Sita might have been harmed by the fire
That Rama would be angry at the delay
That the ocean would dry up
That Sugriva would lose his throne
questions.explanation: Hanuman feared the fire might harm Sita, but learned she remained unburnt and safe.

questions.q: Which book (kanda) of Valmiki's Ramayana centers on Hanuman's journey to Lanka and is named for him in praise?
questions.explanation: The Sundara Kanda recounts Hanuman's leap, search for Sita, and exploits in Lanka.

questions.q: When Ravana ordered Hanuman killed in his court, which of Ravana's brothers argued that an envoy should not be slain?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana counseled that killing a messenger violated dharma, so Hanuman's tail was burned instead.

questions.q: As a child, why did the infant Hanuman leap toward the sun, according to tradition?
options: Mistaking it for a ripe fruit to eat
questions.explanation: The infant Hanuman sprang at the sun thinking it a fruit, which led to Indra striking him.

questions.q: Hanuman's very name is traditionally explained by an injury to which part of his body, struck by Indra's thunderbolt?
questions.explanation: The name Hanuman derives from 'hanu' (jaw), injured by Indra's vajra in childhood.

questions.q: What boon protected Hanuman so that Brahma's weapon (Brahmastra) could bind but not truly harm him in Lanka?
options: Immunity granted by the gods after Indra's blow
questions.explanation: After Indra struck him, the gods granted Hanuman boons making him invulnerable to such weapons, so he submitted to the Brahmastra only to be brought before Ravana.

questions.q: What sign did Sita give Hanuman to carry back to Rama as proof she was alive?
questions.explanation: Sita entrusted Hanuman with her chudamani (hair-jewel) as a token for Rama.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda tradition, what boon did Hanuman receive that makes him a chiranjivi (immortal) devotee?
options: To live as long as Rama's story is told on earth
To command the winds at will
questions.explanation: Hanuman is blessed to remain deathless as long as Rama's name and story endure, embodying eternal devotion.
